title: Curriculum Unavailable

jokes.text: Pierce, all you said was, 'I'm hungry.' / All Henry Ford said was, 'I need a ride.'
jokes.setup: Pierce being called out for taking credit for a simple idea
jokes.punchline: Pierce comparing his basic statement to Henry Ford's innovation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce character moment - grandiose delusions delivered with complete sincerity. The Henry Ford comparison is both absurd and perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I came up with the idea of a blanket fort. It's genius.

dialogue.speaker: Other Character
dialogue.line: Pierce, that's just a blanket over some chairs.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Exactly! You know what Henry Ford said? 'If I had asked people what they wanted, they would have said faster horses.' Well, I didn't ask anyone. I just made a fort.

jokes.text: It's bagel bites in a deconstructed hot pocket reduction with a doritos glaze.
jokes.setup: Someone asking if Troy brought a casserole
jokes.punchline: Troy describing junk food in pretentious culinary terms
jokes.explanation: Troy's childlike approach to cooking described in fancy terms. Good character work but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Oh, this? This is a deconstructed tater tot casserole with a microwave reduction and a Funyun crust, finished with a drizzle of nacho cheese coulis.

jokes.text: Did anybody bring anything that isn't poison?
jokes.setup: Troy's description of his bizarre food creation
jokes.punchline: Shirley's deadpan assessment of the group's potluck contributions
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction from Shirley. Functional but not standout.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Y'all are disgusting. This is what happens when you don't have a black woman in the group.

jokes.text: Pack of lifesavers. Sorry. Dead battery.
jokes.setup: Being asked what he brought to the potluck
jokes.punchline: Jeff's minimal effort and flippant excuse
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff - minimal effort with a nonsensical excuse delivered confidently. Good character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I brought a store-bought pie and told everyone I made it.

jokes.text: See? Cool. Abed brought delicious police.
jokes.setup: Troy saying Abed would bring something cool to dinner
jokes.punchline: Police arrive and Troy maintains Abed brought something 'delicious'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Troy moment - his innocent literalness creates an absurd but perfect description. Very quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Abed's gonna bring something cool to dinner.

dialogue.speaker: Police Officer
dialogue.line: We received a report of an explosive device.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Oh, that's just Abed. He brought something delicious.

jokes.text: This cadet could use a lesson in intergalactic protocol.
jokes.setup: Police officer dealing with Abed in character
jokes.punchline: Officer playing along with the Inspector Spacetime bit
jokes.explanation: Decent moment of the officer engaging with the show's mythology, but not particularly memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Officer
dialogue.line: Alright, freeze! We got a call about suspicious activity.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Inspector Spacetime, reporting for duty.

dialogue.speaker: Officer
dialogue.line: Look, I don't know what you're talking about, but if you could just come with me—

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: The Dregs are here, aren't they? I can feel their temporal distortion.

dialogue.speaker: Officer
dialogue.line: Yeah, sure, buddy. The... Dregs. They're everywhere. We're gonna need to take you down to the precinct—in the Dreamscape.

jokes.text: You think a guy becomes a cop because his prom night was a dream?
jokes.setup: Officer explaining he's familiar with Inspector Spacetime
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating joke about becoming a cop due to social failures
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny and unexpectedly revealing character moment from a minor character. Good writing.

dialogue.speaker: Officer
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'm familiar with Inspector Spacetime. In fact, that's kind of why I became a cop. I figured if I couldn't make it in the Inspector Spacetime fandom, at least I could enforce the law. Turns out I'm not very good at either.

jokes.text: Reggie, to the time booth. We haven't much... space.
jokes.setup: Abed in Inspector Spacetime character
jokes.punchline: The awkward pause before 'space' - clearly struggling with the catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Shows Abed's mental state - even his TV knowledge is becoming fragmented. Good character work.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Insp—... Inspector... Sp—... Space... time.

jokes.text: Note the uneven orange hue and the embossment on the back. What a nifty little piece of americana. You could get 50, 60 bucks for it from the right collector.
jokes.setup: Officer commenting on a brick being used as a doorstop
jokes.punchline: Incredibly detailed antique appraisal of a random brick
jokes.explanation: Completely unexpected character detail. The specificity makes it funny - who appraises random bricks?

dialogue.speaker: Officer
dialogue.line: That's a 1987 clay brick, hand-fired in a kiln in suburban Pennsylvania. The subtle discoloration on the eastern face suggests exposure to approximately 2,000 hours of direct sunlight. Its weight distribution indicates it was likely part of a load-bearing wall in a mid-range residential structure. The mortar residue suggests it was removed sometime between 2003 and 2005. I'd appraise it at roughly $3.50.

jokes.text: I watch a lot of antiques roadshow.
jokes.setup: His detailed brick appraisal
jokes.punchline: Casual explanation for his unlikely expertise
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan explanation. The economy of the line is excellent - explains everything with casual understatement.

dialogue.speaker: Officer
dialogue.line: Well, I appraised bricks for three years before I became a cop.

jokes.text: Anyway, walk to your cars in pairs tonight. Rape's up 8%.
jokes.setup: Casual transition from antique appraisal
jokes.punchline: Jarring shift to disturbing crime statistics delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy gold - the tonal whiplash from antiques to assault statistics is perfectly executed. Very Community.

dialogue.speaker: Officer
dialogue.line: Did you know that every 12 seconds, someone in America is robbed? Every 45 seconds, someone is assaulted. Every 2 minutes, someone is raped. And every 22 minutes, someone is murdered.

jokes.text: Hello? Rich people, Troy's joining you. Yes, I'll hold.
jokes.setup: Learning the brick might be worth $60
jokes.punchline: Troy immediately calling 'rich people' like it's a customer service line
jokes.explanation: Peak Troy childishness - treating wealth like a club you can just call to join. The 'Yes, I'll hold' sells it completely.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Rich people! Rich people! Hello? Yes, I have a brick that's worth sixty dollars. No, I don't know why. Can you come get it? Hello?

jokes.text: He's usually, you know, adorable weird, like mork from ork, but, since we got expelled, he's been creepy weird, like present-day Robin Williams.
jokes.setup: Explaining Abed's condition to the psychiatrist
jokes.punchline: Comparing current Robin Williams to his Mork character as 'creepy weird'
jokes.explanation: Dark but accurate pop culture observation. The specificity of the Robin Williams reference makes it work.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Abed has this thing where he references TV and movies constantly. He sees the world through pop culture.

dialogue.speaker: Psychiatrist
dialogue.line: I see. Can you give me an example?

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Well, like, he'll compare something to Robin Williams. You know, how Robin Williams used to be funny and weird like Mork, but now he's just creepy weird?

jokes.text: So how long has Abed needed a crazy amount of help?
jokes.setup: Claiming she doesn't deal in 'crazy' but 'health'
jokes.punchline: Immediately using 'crazy' in her next question
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay moment showing the psychiatrist's hypocrisy, but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Heidi
dialogue.line: I don't deal in 'crazy,' I deal in health.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Heidi
dialogue.line: So what crazy thing happened?

jokes.text: I 'Rent Tower Heist' last night, and I won't spoil the ending, but let me tell you, it was quite the hilarious Thrill Ride. That Brett Ratner, he's a master at comedic action-adventure. He's just a master at making movies in general. I'm gonna say it. He's the new Spielberg.
jokes.setup: Abed trying to make casual conversation
jokes.punchline: Praising terrible movies and director with obvious forced enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly uncomfortable - Abed trying to be 'normal' by praising bad movies goes against everything we know about his character.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You know what? I've been thinking about Michael Bay. The guy's a visionary. His use of explosions and slow-motion really speaks to the human condition.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: And Transformers? Transformers is cinema. Pure cinema. The way those robots transform really makes you question what it means to be alive.

jokes.text: You're a bad person. You're a bad person.
jokes.setup: His forced praise of Brett Ratner
jokes.punchline: Abed's internal reaction to his own fake enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Great follow-up showing Abed's self-awareness of his performance. The repetition adds to the discomfort.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Brett Ratner is a visionary. His work has redefined cinema. I mean that sincerely.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: *internally* Why am I like this? I can feel myself doing it and I can't stop.

jokes.text: Time enough at last to eat a sandwich. Though even he knew that this sandwich was nearer his last sandwich than his first.
jokes.setup: Abed narrating Pierce eating
jokes.punchline: Twilight Zone parody with unnecessarily dramatic sandwich narration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed behavior - turning mundane moment into TV trope. The Twilight Zone reference is well-executed.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: You're traveling through another dimension. A dimension not only of sight and sound, but of mind. A journey into a wondrous shadow land whose boundaries are that of imagination. That's the signpost up ahead—your next stop: the Twilight Zone.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Pierce Hawthorne sat down to eat a sandwich. An ordinary sandwich on an ordinary day. But this sandwich would change everything.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: He reached for the bread. Two slices of whole wheat. Then came the lettuce. Crisp. Verdant. A sandwich was being born.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Mayonnaise. The condiment of kings. Applied with the precision of a surgeon. Of a artist. Of a man on the edge.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: And then, the turkey. Slice after slice. Each one a testament to his hunger. To his loneliness. To his very existence in this cold, uncaring universe.

jokes.text: He said, oldly, his brittle bones straining to support the weight of his wrinkly skin.
jokes.setup: Continuing to narrate Pierce
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic description of Pierce's age with the made-up adverb 'oldly'
jokes.explanation: The word 'oldly' is perfect - sounds literary but is completely made up. Great continuation of the bit.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Pierce walked into the room oldly, his bones creaking with the weight of his many, many years on this earth.

jokes.text: Pierce Hawthorne had nearly lost his grip on reality. Lately, he'd begun to think he was in a novel.
jokes.setup: Pierce complaining about being narrated
jokes.punchline: Abed describing Pierce thinking he's in a novel while literally being in a TV show
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke - character thinking he's fictional while being fictional, delivered by the most meta-aware character.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Pierce thinks he's the protagonist of some existential novel, completely unaware he's actually trapped in a mediocre TV show with a laugh track.

jokes.text: Abed, it's 4:30 in the morning, and you're in my bedroom. / Use that. Okay. And action.
jokes.setup: Jeff waking up to find Abed filming in his bedroom
jokes.punchline: Abed treating Jeff's genuine complaint as good dialogue for his project
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment showing Abed's disconnect from social boundaries. His response is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Abed, what are you doing in my bedroom at 6 in the morning with a camera?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Your natural confusion and irritation—perfect. Keep going.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: I'm not acting. Get out of my room.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Even better. The denial of performance while performing. This is gold.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Abed, I'm serious. Leave.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: That's the thing though—you're always serious, which makes it funny. Don't you see? You're my muse.

jokes.text: I mean, he's certainly the craziest one of us, but, you know, we're all kind of crazy-town banana pants.
jokes.setup: Defending Abed's sanity
jokes.punchline: Annie's attempt to use colloquial expression 'crazy-town banana pants'
jokes.explanation: Annie trying to be casual with made-up slang. Character-appropriate awkwardness.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Abed is not crazy. He's just... creative. And okay, yes, sometimes he does things that are a little crazy-town banana pants, but that doesn't mean he's actually crazy!

jokes.text: How long does peyote last? Just asking for a friend.
jokes.setup: Discussion about everyone being crazy
jokes.punchline: Pierce's casual drug reference with transparent excuse
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce moment - inappropriate question with obvious lie. Good character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: We're all a little crazy. I mean, I take pills for my back pain, but that's just medicine.

jokes.text: Just don't eat anything or drink anything, and no sweating or bending at the elbows, and no chairs with backrests, and if you take it off, wooden hangers only.
jokes.setup: Annie giving Jeff her coat
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous restrictions for wearing the coat
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Annie's neurotic tendencies. The restrictions get increasingly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: You can borrow my coat, but you have to follow these rules.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: No wearing it on Tuesdays.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: You can't let it touch the ground.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: If someone compliments it, you have to tell them it's mine.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: And you absolutely cannot wear it in a school building, near a school building, or within a two-block radius of a school building.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Also, no sitting in it. Only standing or lying down.

jokes.text: Troy, you can't bring that in here. / Yes, I can. It's all-terrain, dummy.
jokes.setup: Troy apparently bringing something inappropriate indoors
jokes.punchline: Troy's literal interpretation of 'all-terrain' as permission to bring it anywhere
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy logic - completely literal interpretation delivered with confidence. 'Dummy' adds to the humor.

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: Troy! You cannot bring that in here!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: But Shirley, it says right here on the box: 'all-terrain vehicle.' All terrain means all terrain!

dialogue.speaker: Shirley
dialogue.line: That does not mean inside the apartment!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Well technically, this is terrain. I'm standing on it right now.

jokes.text: Karate championship, second place! Now I'll be unstoppable. Oyay!
jokes.setup: Troy giving out his possessions
jokes.punchline: Being excited about second place making him 'unstoppable'
jokes.explanation: Troy's childlike enthusiasm about losing. The 'Oyay!' karate yell is a nice touch.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I'm giving away all my possessions because I came in second place in the dreamers competition.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Second place makes me unstoppable!

jokes.text: Take that back. Our adventures are very manly.
jokes.setup: Being called out for 'little misadventures'
jokes.punchline: Troy's defensive response about their manliness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Troy's insecurity about masculinity, but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Little misadventures? We're men! We go on adventures. Big, manly adventures.

jokes.text: You mean committed to his character work, right? Because he already is. Abed, show him your Don Draper. / Cigarettes.
jokes.setup: Doctor suggesting Abed should be committed to an institution
jokes.punchline: Troy misunderstanding, then Abed's minimal Don Draper impression
jokes.explanation: Good Troy misunderstanding paired with Abed's minimalist impression. The single word response is perfectly deadpan.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Wait, you want to commit Abed to an institution?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I'd like to work in a place where people respect the work.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Abed, that's not what committed means!

jokes.text: You mean like marriage? / I mean a mental institution. / Ah. So do I.
jokes.setup: Doctor clarifying she means mental institution
jokes.punchline: Pierce's misogynistic joke equating marriage with mental institutions
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce - outdated, inappropriate humor that's more character-revealing than funny. Low originality but fits character.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Heidi
dialogue.line: I mean a mental institution.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Oh! Well, that's what I said — marriage!

jokes.text: Will someone please help me lighten the mood?
jokes.setup: Series of dark mental institution jokes
jokes.punchline: Jeff explicitly requests comic relief
jokes.explanation: Meta-awareness of the show's dark turn. Jeff as group's comedy traffic controller.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Okay, can we please get some comic relief here? This is getting dark.

jokes.text: Please, Mr. Dr. psychiatrist, sir, please don't send my best friend to crazy people jail.
jokes.setup: Troy pleading for Abed
jokes.punchline: Describing mental institution as 'crazy people jail' with excessive formality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy - childish terminology combined with overly formal address. Very quotable and character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Please, you have to believe me. Abed isn't crazy, he just processes the world differently!

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: A mental institution is not a... a crazy people jail. It's a facility for psychiatric care and treatment.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: I'm speaking with the utmost formality and gravity here.

jokes.text: Yeah, he's just unique, like a snowflake who gets bent out of shape when you mix up Star Wars and space treks. / Or a serial killer's signature.
jokes.setup: Annie's innocent comparison of Abed to a snowflake
jokes.punchline: Britta's dark psychology major interpretation
jokes.explanation: Excellent contrast between Annie's sweetness and Britta's psychology major darkness. Perfect timing and character work.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Abed is like a snowflake - unique and beautiful.

dialogue.speaker: Britta
dialogue.line: Or he's cold, isolated, and will eventually melt into nothing, which is basically what happens to all of us.

jokes.text: We had lockers. / We're survivors.
jokes.setup: Defending their Greendale experience
jokes.punchline: Treating having lockers as evidence of surviving trauma
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd logic - lockers as proof of institutional trauma. The economy is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: We survived Greendale. We have the scars to prove it.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: We had lockers.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Exactly. Lockers.

jokes.text: My name is Pwofussa Wobinswin. Who's weady to wearn?
jokes.setup: Flashback to bizarre Greendale moment
jokes.punchline: Professor with apparent speech impediment making learning announcement
jokes.explanation: Cheap speech impediment humor, but fits the show's absurdist tone about Greendale being weird.

dialogue.speaker: Professor
dialogue.line: Attention students, I have an important announcement about the upcoming midterm examination.

jokes.text: Vicki to Queen 3. / Neigh.
jokes.setup: Apparent chess game at Greendale
jokes.punchline: Chess notation followed by horse sound, suggesting human chess with costumes
jokes.explanation: Clever visual gag - human chess game revealed through sound effect. Good Community-style absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Knight to E4.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Neigh!

jokes.text: Get out. There's one every year. I will not tolerate clowning.
jokes.setup: Student apparently asking about inappropriate food item
jokes.punchline: Stern rejection of someone trying to be funny about food
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing cafeteria worker's no-nonsense attitude. 'One every year' suggests this happens regularly.

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: Can I get a blowjob shot?

dialogue.speaker: Cafeteria Worker
dialogue.line: No. And stop trying to make that a thing.

jokes.text: Slow your bones, Rufus. We got a long road tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Someone trying to rush past
jokes.punchline: Old-timey cowboy dialogue in modern college setting
jokes.explanation: Random anachronistic dialogue that fits Greendale's weirdness. 'Slow your bones' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: Welcome... to ladders.
jokes.setup: Dramatic pause suggesting important announcement
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic introduction to mundane subject
jokes.explanation: Perfect Community absurdity - treating ladders as worthy of dramatic introduction. The pause makes it funnier.

jokes.text: How are you? / Why do you ask? 3:30. / Uh-oh.
jokes.setup: Simple question about how Pierce is doing
jokes.punchline: Pierce's paranoid response and Annie recognizing 'sundowning' time
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Pierce's age-related issues. Annie's recognition adds to the humor.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Heidi
dialogue.line: How are you doing today, Pierce?

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: Well, I'll tell you, Doc. Every time I close my eyes, I see faces. Angry faces. And they're all screaming my name. But not in a good way, you know? More like a 'we're gonna get you' way.

dialogue.speaker: Annie
dialogue.line: Oh, it's sundowning time.

jokes.text: I just wanted to drop by and tell you we ought to be hot-stepping it out to the parking lot in a brisk, but orderly fashion.
jokes.setup: Dean announcing something urgent
jokes.punchline: Overly cheerful, verbose way of saying 'evacuate quickly'
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean verbosity - turning simple evacuation into cheerful corporate-speak. Good character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Everyone, I need you to exit the building in a prompt yet orderly fashion with the utmost urgency while maintaining a positive attitude and perhaps even smiling as we vacate these premises immediately!

jokes.text: I wanted you guys to be the first to know before I tell everyone else. I know. It's not right to play favorites, but it is no more right to sit on your feelings, and I don't know what I'd do without you guys.
jokes.setup: Dean announcing fire emergency
jokes.punchline: Getting emotional and making fire evacuation about his personal feelings for the group
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dean moment - inappropriate emotional oversharing during emergency. Shows his obsession with the group.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Everyone, we need to evacuate immediately! This is a fire emergency!

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: And I just want you all to know... before we potentially never see each other again... you've meant so much to me. Each and every one of you.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Jeff, your confidence and leadership... Britta, your passion... Troy, your innocence... Abed, your uniqueness... Annie, your dedication... Shirley, your warmth... Pierce, your... presence.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: And I hope that if we survive this, you'll remember that your dean cares about you!

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Now GO! Before the fire consumes us all! And please think of me fondly!

jokes.text: Darcy, you've got a cold. I feel fine. You're sick. Go home! Get out of here. Get out of here. Get out of here!
jokes.setup: Dean supposedly caring for sick student
jokes.punchline: Aggressive, repeated demands for student to leave
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Dean's manic energy, but not exceptional. The repetition works.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: I'm here to check on you. How are you feeling?

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: You need to leave. Get out of here.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: I said get out! Out! OUT!

jokes.text: Psst, it's me, the Dean. I pulled the last six pieces of pizza for you.
jokes.setup: Students missing pizza day
jokes.punchline: Dean secretly hoarding pizza for his favorite students
jokes.explanation: Sweet Dean moment showing his favoritism. The stealth approach is endearing and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Pizza day has been cancelled due to... unforeseen circumstances.

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: But Dean, we were looking forward to it!

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: I know, I know. It's just that I've decided to implement a new policy: pizza is now reserved exclusively for my most... academically promising students.

dialogue.speaker: Student
dialogue.line: That's not fair!

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: Life isn't fair. But my office is very fair. And very well-stocked with pepperoni.

jokes.text: ♪ Troy and Jeffrey and Annie and Britta and Shirley and Pierce and Abed and Jeffrey just a few of the most special students in the world ♪
jokes.setup: Dean's emotional attachment to the group
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top song about how special they are, repeating Jeffrey
jokes.explanation: Dean's over-emotional attachment expressed through song. Repeating Jeffrey adds to the manic quality.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: You are the best study group, the best study group in the whole world. You're so special, so special, Jeffrey loves you so much. Jeffrey loves you, Jeffrey loves you, Jeffrey loves you so much.

jokes.text: It's not important anymore. I get it now. It just feels forced.
jokes.setup: Dean saying paintball is important to Abed
jokes.punchline: Abed's meta-commentary on the show's own paintball episodes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on the show itself. Abed acknowledging that paintball episodes have become repetitive - peak Community self-awareness.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Paintball is important to me because it's the only time this show becomes interesting.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Also, it's basically just an excuse for the writers to do a action movie parody while ignoring character development for 40 minutes.

jokes.text: Lame!
jokes.setup: Dean offers prize of community theater tickets with aging celebrities
jokes.punchline: Students' blunt dismissal of terrible prize
jokes.explanation: Perfect anticlimax. The economy and timing make a simple reaction effective.

dialogue.speaker: Dean Pelton
dialogue.line: The prize is two tickets to see the community theater production of 'Cats' featuring special guest appearances by... well, let's just say some very famous people from the '70s.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Winger
dialogue.line: Pass.

dialogue.speaker: Britta Perry
dialogue.line: Hard pass.

dialogue.speaker: Troy Barnes
dialogue.line: No thank you.

dialogue.speaker: Abed Nadir
dialogue.line: I'd rather watch the actual cats.

jokes.text: Ah, yes, this fantastical community college where everything that happens is unbelievably ridiculous and it all revolves around you as a group.
jokes.setup: Jeff's defense of community college as mundane
jokes.punchline: Describing the show's actual absurdist premise as evidence of delusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-commentary on the show's own absurdist nature. The fake psychiatrist accurately describes Community's format.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Winger
dialogue.line: Community college isn't some magical place where—

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Heidi
dialogue.line: Jeff, you've fought a paintball war, joined a cult, and your study group has stopped an actual conspiracy. You once had to negotiate with a dementia patient who thought she was a secret agent.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff Winger
dialogue.line: Okay, but that's just—

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Heidi
dialogue.line: You're not delusional about community college being mundane. You're delusional if you think any of that was normal.

jokes.text: I'm a millionaire, I'm an inventor, I'm a legend, and I had sex with Eartha Kitt.
jokes.setup: Pierce listing his accomplishments in the 'delusion'
jokes.punchline: Escalating claims ending with specific celebrity encounter
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce grandiose claims with callback to his recurring Eartha Kitt story. Good escalation structure.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I've accomplished more than most men. I've built a business empire, I've won awards, I've been recognized by my peers.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: I've dined with senators, I've golfed with CEOs, I've— I once made eye contact with Clint Eastwood at an airport.

dialogue.speaker: Pierce
dialogue.line: He nodded at me. A full nod. Not just a glance—a nod.

jokes.text: Should we increase their lithium? / Not yet. I want to see what happens if we confiscate one of their pens.
jokes.setup: Discussion of patient treatment
jokes.punchline: Treating pen confiscation as psychological experiment
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about psychiatric treatment. The mundane nature of the 'experiment' makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Heidi
dialogue.line: We need to confiscate his pens.

dialogue.speaker: Nurse
dialogue.line: Isn't that a bit extreme?

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Heidi
dialogue.line: It's not confiscation, it's a psychological experiment. We're studying the effects of pen deprivation on patient behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Nurse
dialogue.line: You're just calling it an experiment so it sounds scientific.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Heidi
dialogue.line: Exactly. That's what makes me a doctor.

jokes.text: Mi hambre es bombero tirantes.
jokes.setup: Asked if anyone speaks Spanish after three years of Spanish study group
jokes.punchline: Jeff's nonsensical Spanish that translates roughly to 'My hunger is firefighter suspenders'
jokes.explanation: Perfect nonsensical Spanish that sounds like it could be real but makes no sense. Reveals their study group's failure.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: Mi hambre es... bombero... suspensores.

jokes.text: And you shared this delusion with each other, like that time all those people got into swing dance music back in the '90s.
jokes.setup: Explaining shared psychosis
jokes.punchline: Comparing group delusion to swing dance revival
jokes.explanation: Clever comparison that makes shared delusion sound like a fad. The specificity of swing dance makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Heidi
dialogue.line: Shared psychosis is a rare psychiatric syndrome where delusional beliefs are transmitted from one individual to another.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Heidi
dialogue.line: It's much like how the swing dance revival of the 1990s spread through social groups with no rational basis whatsoever.

jokes.text: Because, Ms. Edison, Greendale is purgatory, and I am the devil.
jokes.setup: Being asked why she would lie to them
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top dramatic revelation
jokes.explanation: Melodramatic reveal that's intentionally over-the-top. Works as parody of dramatic twists.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Heidi
dialogue.line: Because I'm not who you think I am!

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Heidi
dialogue.line: I'm actually a deep-cover operative for the Belgian government, and I've been using Greendale as a front to smuggle artisanal waffles into the country!

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Heidi
dialogue.line: Everything you know is a lie. My name isn't even Heidi. It's... Brunhilde.

jokes.text: I'm a fake psychiatrist, not a fake ethnologist. / He's Arabic. / Also Polish.
jokes.setup: Fake doctor getting Abed's ethnicity wrong
jokes.punchline: Admitting to being fake while getting the correction, then Abed's addition
jokes.explanation: Good joke about fake expertise extending to multiple fields. Abed's additional correction is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Heidi
dialogue.line: So what brings you in today?

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I'm not feeling well.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Heidi
dialogue.line: Hmm, yes. Well, as a Persian doctor, I can tell you...

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: I'm actually half-Iraqi, half-Polish.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Heidi
dialogue.line: Oh. Well, I should probably tell you I'm not actually a doctor.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: Yeah, and I'm not actually sick. I just wanted to see if you'd figure it out.

jokes.text: Ms. Edison, Greendale is purgatory, and I am the devil.
jokes.setup: Doctor pressed for better explanation
jokes.punchline: Completely ridiculous metaphysical claim
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation when caught in lies. Good comedic commitment to increasingly ridiculous claims.

jokes.text: What's phase two? / Hey, I'm making up the same plan you are.
jokes.setup: Assistant asking about the next phase
jokes.punchline: Chang admitting he's improvising the plan as they go
jokes.explanation: Good Chang moment showing his incompetence as a villain. The honesty about making it up is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Assistant
dialogue.line: So what's the next phase of your plan?

dialogue.speaker: Chang
dialogue.line: Well, I'm gonna be honest with you. I'm kind of making this up as I go.

jokes.text: Troy, why don't we open up the phone lines? / Talk to us, lonely hearts.
jokes.setup: Troy and Abed doing their morning show at night
jokes.punchline: Treating it like a late-night call-in show for lonely people
jokes.explanation: Good callback to their recurring show bit. The lonely hearts angle fits the late-night setting.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: Welcome back to Subway, where we serve sandwiches and loneliness.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: We're taking calls tonight from people who have nowhere else to be.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: First caller, you're on the air. Tell us about your evening.

dialogue.speaker: Abed
dialogue.line: And please, make it sadder than our sandwiches.

jokes.text: may your dreams be sweet and your nightmares be 'spooky monster' scary and not 'grandma died' scary.
jokes.setup: Troy giving nighttime sign-off
jokes.punchline: Distinguishing between fun scary and real-life scary nightmares
jokes.explanation: Beautiful Troy wisdom - perfectly childlike distinction between types of scary. Sweet sentiment delivered with perfect Troy logic.

dialogue.speaker: Troy
dialogue.line: And that's our show. But before you go to bed tonight, I want you to think about the difference between fun scary and real-life scary. Fun scary is like, a nightmare where you're being chased by a clown. Real-life scary is like, a nightmare where you're not being chased by a clown, but you're still worried about your student loans.

tmdb_overview: After Abed is caught trespassing at Greendale, he and his friends visit a psychiatrist.
insight: Absurdist character comedy sustains an 81.5 score despite zero standout punchlines—pure consistency wins.
